title: 10 best herbal capsules for tooth decay
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for tooth decay are natural supplements that help prevent and treat tooth decay by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and promoting oral health. The best herbal capsules for tooth decay include Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Salvia officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Trifolium pratense, Syzygium aromaticum, Sanguinaria canadensis, Aloe barbadensis, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, tooth decay can lead to painful toothaches, tooth loss, and even affect overall health, impacting daily life, social interactions, and work productivity, ultimately affecting one's confidence and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with tooth decay because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause cavities. The capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce the formation of plaque and prevent tartar buildup. Additionally, the antioxidants present in pot marigold help to neutralize free radicals that can damage tooth enamel, promoting a healthier smile. By supplementing with herbal pot marigold capsules, individuals can support their oral health and reduce their risk of tooth decay and other dental problems.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Pot Marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Pot Marigold have anti-inflammatory effects, which may reduce inflammation and pain associated with tooth decay, as well as promoting healing and tissue repair.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants in Pot Marigold capsules can help neutralize free radicals that damage teeth and contribute to tooth decay, thereby protecting the oral cavity from oxidative stress.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Calendula officinalis have antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce the growth of bacteria that contribute to tooth decay.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect the gums and oral tissues from damage caused by plaque and bacteria.
Carotenoids: Carotenoids like beta-carotene may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, potentially aiding in the prevention of tooth decay and gum disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat tooth decay.
Leaves: The leaves contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help prevent tooth decay.
Seeds: The seeds are a rich source of triterpenoids and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Dry the calendula flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 2 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove lumps and large particles.
Mix the sifted powder with gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:1 by weight.
Fill the capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacteria that cause cavities. The capsules' natural oils, such as melaleuca alternifolia, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans and other bacteria that contribute to tooth decay. Additionally, tea tree's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain associated with dental issues, making it a holistic and effective way to promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Tea Tree oil's antimicrobial property helps to eliminate bacteria that can cause tooth decay by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from multiplying, thereby reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of Tea Tree oil reduces inflammation in the mouth, which is often associated with tooth decay, and helps to alleviate gum irritation, swelling, and pain.
Antioxidant property: Tea Tree oil's antioxidant property neutralizes free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, protecting against oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Terpinen-4-ol: This terpene has antibacterial properties that help combat the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary contributor to tooth decay.
Cineol (Eucalyptol): This compound exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing plaque formation and preventing the progression of tooth decay.
Cymene: This terpene has been shown to possess antimicrobial activity against oral pathogens, such as Streptococcus mutans, thereby helping to prevent tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain high amounts of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties that help prevent tooth decay and gum disease.
Stems: The stems also contain tea tree oil, which has been used to reduce plaque, prevent tooth decay, and fight gum disease.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in preventing tooth decay and gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried leaves of melaleuca alternifolia from a clean and sterile environment.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 10g of starch and 5g of cellulose in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 5 minutes.
Label and date 100 capsules with the contents and preparation date for future reference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with tooth decay because of its natural antibacterial properties that combat the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a common bacteria responsible for cavity formation. The antioxidants present in sage capsules also help to neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the production of acid-forming enzymes, thereby preventing the breakdown of tooth enamel and reducing the risk of tooth decay.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage contains compounds like thymol and camphor that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to tooth decay and other oral health issues.

Antioxidant properties: Sage is rich in antioxidants like vitamin E and polyphenols that can help neutralize free radicals and protect teeth from oxidative damage, which can contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties that help inhibit the growth of bacteria causing tooth decay, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce plaque formation and inflammation associated with tooth decay.

Salvianolic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to tooth decay by damaging tooth enamel and promoting bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria responsible for tooth decay.
Flowers: Used for their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the degradation of tooth enamel.
remedy_recipe: Dry 30g of salvia officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powdered salvia officinalis with 1g of a binding agent such as cellulose.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the mixture and seal them immediately.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has antibacterial properties. Berberine inhibits the growth of Streptococcus mutans, the bacteria responsible for causing cavities and tooth decay. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment. By reducing bacterial growth and inflammation, goldenseal capsules may help prevent or alleviate tooth decay, making them a valuable supplement in maintaining good oral health.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key alkaloid found in Goldenseal, has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for tooth decay and gum disease.

Berbanamine: Berbanamine, another important constituent of Goldenseal, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to tooth decay and gum disease.

Cinchonine: Cinchonine, a bioactive compound in Goldenseal, has antibacterial and antiviral properties that help combat bacterial infections and viruses associated with tooth decay, such as Streptococcus mutans.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine, an alkaloid, has antimicrobial properties that help inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, which are commonly associated with tooth decay.

Hydrastine: Hydrastine, an alkaloid, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce plaque formation and gum inflammation, thereby contributing to the prevention of tooth decay.

Vasconine: Vasconine, a phenolic glycoside, has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help protect the oral cavity from oxidative stress and bacterial infections, which can contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their antibacterial properties and ability to prevent the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay.
Leaves: Contain berberine, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of oral bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Used to create extracts that have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the development of tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of hydrastis canadensis roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature.
Dry the roots at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours in a temperature-controlled environment.
Grind 2 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of powdered hydrastis canadensis root using a capsule filler.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent antimicrobial properties. The active ingredients in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay, including Streptococcus mutans. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and plaque formation in the mouth, further preventing tooth decay.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that cause tooth decay, reducing the risk of cavities and infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to tooth decay and other oral health issues.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage teeth and surrounding tissues, helping to prevent tooth decay and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help to combat the bacteria that cause tooth decay and gum disease, ultimately promoting oral health.

Shogaols: These compounds have been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth, thus contributing to the prevention of tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help in preventing tooth decay and gum diseases.
Barks: The barks of the Zingiber officinale contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which help in preventing tooth decay and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with a sharp knife and clean them thoroughly with water for 5 minutes.
Dry the rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture slowly.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 mg of the ground powder with 250 mg of a natural filler like rice flour in a bowl carefully.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder-filler mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the active ingredients effectively.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cavities. Additionally, licorice root extract has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce plaque formation and gum inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for oral health. By taking herbal licorice capsules regularly, individuals can maintain healthy teeth and gums, reducing the risk of tooth decay and other oral health issues.
remedy_properties: Triterpenoids: Herbal licorice capsules contain triterpenoids, which have antibacterial properties that help inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has been shown to reduce plaque formation and prevent bacterial adhesion to teeth, thereby reducing the risk of tooth decay.
Flavonoids: Licorice capsules also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect against free radicals that can damage teeth and contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids, particularly glabridin and glabrene, have been found to have antibacterial properties that help combat the bacteria responsible for tooth decay, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the gums, which can be a contributing factor to tooth decay.
Liquiritin: Liquiritin, a triterpenoid saponin, has been found to have antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, which can lead to tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra to make capsules for tooth decay because they contain high concentrations of glycyrrhizin, a compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make capsules for tooth decay due to their content of flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for tooth decay because they contain saponins, which have been found to have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable source.
Dry the roots further at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours in a low-temperature oven.
Grind 10-15 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 5-7 grams of gelatin and 10-15 ml of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain mucilage, a natural protective substance that soothes and shields the gums and teeth from bacteria. When taken orally, slippery elm capsules can help to reduce inflammation and prevent the buildup of plaque, which can lead to cavities and tooth decay. Additionally, slippery elm's antimicrobial properties may help to combat bacteria that contribute to tooth decay, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm capsules contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can soothe and calm oral tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with tooth decay.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of slippery elm may help reduce the production of free radicals in the mouth, which can contribute to the development of tooth decay by damaging teeth and gums.

Wound healing promotion: Slippery elm's mucilages and antioxidants may also promote wound healing by providing a protective barrier for damaged tissues and enhancing the body's natural healing processes.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to tooth decay.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ulmus rubra may help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of dental plaque, thereby minimizing the risk of tooth decay.

Tannins: The tannins present in Ulmus rubra may have astringent properties that can help reduce bacterial adhesion to tooth surfaces, thereby preventing the accumulation of bacteria that lead to tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make capsules due to its astringent properties that may help in reducing inflammation and preventing tooth decay.
Roots: Used to make capsules due to its antimicrobial properties that may help in reducing bacteria that cause tooth decay.
Leaves: Used to make capsules due to its antioxidant properties that may help in preventing oxidative stress that can lead to tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and bark in the morning after dew has evaporated but before intense heat.
Dry the harvested ulmus rubra leaves and bark in a well-ventilated area at 40°c for 2 hours.
Crush the dried ulmus rubra leaves and bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ulmus rubra powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of distilled water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared ulmus rubra mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause cavities. Curcumin also promotes healthy gums and reduces inflammation, which can contribute to tooth decay. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect teeth from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of tooth decay. Regular use of turmeric capsules may help prevent and even reverse early stages of tooth decay.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay by reducing plaque formation and inhibiting biofilm development.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to tooth decay, gum disease, and other oral health issues.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in turmeric capsules help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can weaken tooth enamel and increase the risk of tooth decay.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps prevent tooth decay by reducing the formation of plaque and inhibiting the growth of oral bacteria.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid with antimicrobial properties, which helps combat tooth decay by suppressing the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary contributor to dental caries.
Turmerone: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which helps mitigate tooth decay by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes in the oral cavity.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including curcumin, which has antimicrobial properties that help prevent tooth decay.
Roots: Roots are used for their antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can lead to tooth decay.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can prevent tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Grind 250 grams of dried curcuma longa roots into fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of purified water in a bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat in a saucepan for 10 minutes or until the gelatin dissolves.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Fill 20 empty gelatin capsules with 1/4 teaspoon of the thickened mixture each using a scoop.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain a powerful antioxidant called aloin, which has antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria that cause cavities. The aloin in the capsules also stimulates saliva production, which helps to neutralize acids and remineralize teeth, reducing the risk of tooth decay. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera help reduce inflammation and plaque, further protecting against tooth decay and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain in gums and teeth, making it easier to manage tooth decay-related discomfort.
Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial agents, such as acemannan, inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to tooth decay, preventing further damage and promoting healing.
Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that accelerate wound healing, including the repair of tooth enamel and gum tissue damaged by tooth decay.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is an anthraquinone compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacteria associated with tooth decay.
Alizarin: Alizarin is a phenolic compound that has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the formation of plaque and reduce the risk of tooth decay.

Saponins: Saponins are a type of triterpenoid that have been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the risk of tooth decay by preventing the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain a high concentration of aloe-emodin and aloin, which have antibacterial properties that help prevent tooth decay.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent tooth decay.

Aloe vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract from leaves contains a compound called aloe-annosum, which has antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature aloe vera leaves with yellow tips and a firm texture on the plant.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or impurities from the surface.
Extract the gel from the leaves using a juicer or blender and strain the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 500 mg of vitamin e oil and 100 mg of ginseng extract in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the aloe vera mixture using an encapsulation machine and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain a powerful antimicrobial compound called eucalyptol, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to tooth decay. Additionally, the capsules also contain other natural compounds such as menthol and camphor, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce plaque formation and prevent cavities from developing.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Eucalyptus oil in the capsules has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, which is a common cause of tooth decay, preventing the formation of cavities.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums and mouth tissues, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with tooth decay, such as pain and sensitivity.
Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect teeth from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for tooth decay, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Flavonoids (including Quercetin): Exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can damage tooth enamel and contribute to the development of tooth decay.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content, which contains compounds with antibacterial properties that help prevent tooth decay.
Barks: The barks are used as they contain antioxidants and compounds that help reduce inflammation, promoting oral health and preventing tooth decay.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus are used due to their essential oil content, which has antimicrobial properties that help prevent bacterial growth and reduce the risk of tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted supplier or cultivate your own.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 500 milligrams of a carrier like rice flour in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the eucalyptus globulus mixture using a small spoon and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem capsules helps with tooth decay because of its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for cavities. The capsules contain compounds like azadirachtin and salannin, which have been shown to reduce plaque formation and prevent tartar buildup on teeth. Additionally, neem's antibacterial properties help to kill Streptococcus mutans, a bacterium commonly associated with tooth decay. By targeting the source of the problem, neem capsules provide a natural and effective way to combat tooth decay and promote healthy oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Azadirachtin: This compound has antibacterial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing tooth decay, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Limonoene: This terpene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help combat the oral pathogens responsible for tooth decay, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy gums.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to tooth decay and other oral health issues.

Barks: Neem barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, making them effective against dental plaque and cavities.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried neem leaves, one cup of dried amla powder and 250 ml of ghee.
Boil the mixture in 500 ml of water for 15 minutes to create a potent decoction.
Allow the decoction to cool down and then add 100 grams of gelatin powder.
Mix well until the gelatin is fully dissolved and then fill small capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried vitis vinifera leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Grind the vitis vinifera plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered vitis vinifera with 125 grams of gum arabic powder in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the vitis vinifera-gum arabic mixture and seal securely.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric capsules helps with tooth decay because of its antimicrobial properties that combat harmful bacteria in the mouth. remedy_recipe: Collect berberis aristata roots and dry them for at least 7 days under shade to remove moisture.
Grind the dried berberis aristata roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the powdered berberis aristata root with 500mg of natural binder such as gum acacia in a bowl for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to prevent exposure to air and moisture for 20 seconds.
Store the prepared herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain a natural antibacterial property called cinnamaldehyde, which inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria that contribute to tooth decay. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh cinnamomum verum bark from sustainably sourced trees.
Dry the harvested bark in a cool dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 500g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground cinnamomum verum with 2 parts gelatin in a bowl to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with tooth decay because they contain compounds that have potent antibacterial properties. Specifically, thymol, a bioactive compound found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacterium responsible for many cases of tooth decay. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Grind dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered thymus vulgaris with 500mg of gelatin capsules and 100mg of calcium carbonate.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine or spoon and carefully close each capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley capsules helps with tooth decay because of their natural antibacterial properties. The capsule's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cavities and gum disease. Additionally, parsley's antimicrobial properties help to reduce plaque formation, preventing the buildup of acidic substances that contribute to tooth decay. By taking herbal parsley capsules regularly, individuals can support their oral health by reducing the risk of tooth decay and promoting a healthy, disease-free mouth.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Parsley capsules contain flavonoids, which have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for tooth decay and gum disease, preventing further damage.
Vitamins A and C: The high content of vitamins A and C in parsley capsules helps maintain healthy gums, teeth, and dental tissues, reducing the risk of tooth decay and promoting overall oral health.

Folate: Folate, a B vitamin present in parsley capsules, plays a crucial role in the synthesis of collagen, which is essential for maintaining the strength and structure of teeth and gums, preventing decay and damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing plaque and preventing tooth decay.
Seeds: Containing volatile oils with antimicrobial properties, they help combat bacteria that contribute to tooth decay.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 24 hours or until they become crumbly.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground petroselinum crispum powder with 250ml of distilled water to create an infusion that steeps for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the petroselinum crispum powder and seal tightly after preparation time.
